author: Tony Boules
description: Import Supernote digest backups and convert your highlighted digests into notes in your Obsidian vault, organized as atomic or per-document notes. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
1.0.6

  This release supersedes 1.0.5, which was published without its build assets attached — 1.0.6 is the first version with a working download.
  It addresses feedback from the Obsidian community plugin review: no user-facing feature changes, but several fixes to code quality,
  security posture, and one reliability improvement.

  Fixes & improvements

  - Note linking is now non-destructive. Previously, when a new digest came in that continued a document you'd already generated notes for,
  the plugin would delete and regenerate the last note to update its "next" link — any manual edits you'd made to that note would be lost.
  Now the previous/next links live in frontmatter and are updated in place; your notes and any edits to them are never deleted.
  - Faster, safer backup parsing. Replaced the JSZip library with fflate, removing a flagged dynamic script-injection pattern from the
  bundled code and only decompressing the backup entries actually needed for new digests, instead of the whole archive up front.
  - File deletion now respects your trash preference. Notes removed when switching organization styles go through Obsidian's
  FileManager.trashFile() instead of being permanently deleted outright. This raises the plugin's minimum supported Obsidian version to
  1.6.6.
  - Cleaned up unsafe type handling around file-path resolution and backup JSON parsing.
  - Replaced manual DOM creation with Obsidian's own UI helpers (Setting().setHeading(), createEl()) for section headers and the backup file
  picker.
  - Renamed leftover sample-plugin class/interface names to match this plugin.
  - Release builds now include GitHub artifact attestations for main.js, so the published build can be verified against this source.

  No changes to settings, note structure, or generated content — a re-import of an existing backup should behave the same as before, just
  without the destructive reprocessing step.

releases.description:
1.0.5

This release addresses feedback from the Obsidian community plugin review — no user-facing feature changes, but several fixes to
code quality, security posture, and one reliability improvement.

Fixes & improvements

Note linking is now non-destructive. Previously, when a new digest came in that continued a document you'd already generated notes
for, the plugin would delete and regenerate the last note to update its "next" link — any manual edits you'd made to that note
would be lost. Now the previous/next links live in frontmatter and are updated in place; your notes and any edits to them are never
deleted.
Faster, safer backup parsing. Replaced the JSZip library with fflate, removing a flagged dynamic script-injection pattern from the
bundled code and only decompressing the backup entries actually needed for new digests, instead of the whole archive up front.
File deletion now respects your trash preference. Notes removed when switching organization styles go through Obsidian's
FileManager.trashFile() instead of being permanently deleted outright.
Cleaned up unsafe type handling around file-path resolution and backup JSON parsing.
Replaced manual DOM creation with Obsidian's own UI helpers (Setting().setHeading(), createEl()) for section headers and the
backup file picker.
Renamed leftover sample-plugin class/interface names to match this plugin.
Release builds now include GitHub artifact attestations for main.js, so the published build can be verified against this source.
No changes to settings, note structure, or generated content — a re-import of an existing backup should behave the same as before,
just without the destructive reprocessing step.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Supernote Obsidian Digest — v1.0.0

  Import your Supernote digest backups directly into your Obsidian vault — no manual export or PDF extraction needed.

  ### Features
  - Upload a Supernote `.snbak` backup and automatically generate notes from your highlighted digests
  - Choose your note organization style:
    - **Atomic** — each digest becomes its own note, linked into an Atlas (map of content) per source document
    - **Document** — all digests for a source document are collected into a single note
  - Handwritten mark images are extracted and embedded alongside each digest, with trailing whitespace trimmed automatically
  - Re-uploading a backup only creates notes for new digests — existing notes are left untouched
  - Configurable paths for digests, images, and atlas folders
  - Progress bar while processing large backups
  - The plugin's atlas templates rely on the Dataview community plugin

  ### Requirements
  - Obsidian 1.5.7 or later
  - Desktop only

  ### Installation
  This plugin has been submitted to the Community Plugins directory — once approved, you'll be able to install it directly from
  Settings → Community plugins by searching for "Supernote Obsidian Digest".

  In the meantime, or for manual installation: download `main.js` and `manifest.json` from this release and place them in
  `<YourVault>/.obsidian/plugins/supernote-obsidian-digest/`, then enable the plugin in Settings → Community plugins.
